thinking.text:
Based on the knowledge base, I found the relevant information about why referrals might be rejected. Looking at the user's referral history:

1. Bronze Rewards Card - COMPLETE - 10/20/2025
2. Gold Rewards Card - COMPLETE - 10/22/2025
3. Silver Rewards Card - IN_PROGRESS - 11/05/2025
4. Platinum Rewards Card - REJECTED - 10/25/2025

The key information from the knowledge base is:

For credit card referrals:
- "You can receive at most 2 referral bonuses in any rolling 7-day window."
- "If you attempt to receive more than 2 referrals in a single week, the third referral (and any subsequent referrals that week) will be automatically denied."

Looking at the dates:
- Bronze Rewards Card: 10/20/2025 (COMPLETE)
- Gold Rewards Card: 10/22/2025 (COMPLETE)
- Platinum Rewards Card: 10/25/2025 (REJECTED)

The Platinum referral on 10/25/2025 was only 3 days after the Gold referral on 10/22/2025, and only 5 days after the Bronze referral on 10/20/2025. Since the user had already received 2 referral bonuses (Bronze and Gold) within the 7-day rolling window, the Platinum referral was automatically rejected due to the weekly limit.

This is the most likely reason for the rejection. I should explain this to the user.

worker_attempts.visible_answer:
Based on your referral history and our credit card referral policy, I can explain what happened with your Platinum Rewards Card referral.

**The reason for the rejection: Weekly Referral Limit**

Our credit card referral program has a **rolling 7-day window limit** — you can receive a maximum of **2 referral bonuses in any 7-day period**.

Looking at your referral timeline:
- **Bronze Rewards Card** — Completed on 10/20/2025
- **Gold Rewards Card** — Completed on 10/22/2025
- **Platinum Rewards Card** — Rejected on 10/25/2025

Your Platinum referral on 10/25/2025 was only **3 days** after your Gold referral and **5 days** after your Bronze referral. Since you had already received 2 successful referral bonuses within that 7-day window, the third referral was automatically denied.

**What this means:**
- The rejection was due to timing, not anything wrong with the referral itself
- The limit resets on a rolling basis — once your oldest qualifying referral is more than 7 days old, you become eligible again

**Your Silver Rewards Card referral** (11/05/2025) is currently **IN PROGRESS**, which means it's outside that 7-day window and is proceeding normally.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your referrals or the program?
